sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Larry, a 60-year-old male, has been experiencing chronic back pain for the past couple of years, which started after lifting furniture one weekend.
He describes the pain as sharp stabbing and radiating down to his right leg and big toe.
He also reports numbness and tingling in his right leg, particularly in the right lateral thigh at L4 dermatome.
He denies loss of sensation in the genital or rectal area, weakness, loss of bladder or bowel control, and any family history of spine conditions.
He has tried physical therapy, pain medications, and has been advised of surgery, but prefers to avoid it.
He has gained weight due to his back pain and has difficulty being active.
He used to enjoy playing golf but has had to stop due to his condition.
Past medical history: Not mentioned.
Review of systems: The patient denies any other symptoms.
Current medications: Not mentioned.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The musculoskeletal back exam revealed no overlying erythema or ecchymosis, but midline tenderness at L4-L5 disk space with right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness.
The patient experienced pain with lumbar flexion and a positive straight leg test.
The neurological examination revealed decreased sensation to light touch at the right lateral thigh at L4 dermatome, but symmetrical patellar and Achilles reflexes.
Test Results: The MRI results show disk herniation with nerve impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are consistent with an L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy, causing pain and numbness in the right leg.
Problem list: Lumbar back pain; L4-L5 herniated disk; Radiculopathy; Obesity.
Plan:
I prescribed a corticosteroid injection to the lumbar spine under fluoroscopy to help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
I will monitor the patient's response to the injection and schedule follow-up appointments as needed.
The patient will not require any ongoing treatment, but may need up to four injections per year.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a follow-up appointment to assess the effectiveness of the corticosteroid injection and to schedule additional injections as needed.
